title: intense icy mexican chicken green matcha flan
description: a refreshing and intense dessert inspired by the flavors of jalisco, mexico, featuring matcha and a silky smooth texture.
instructions: roast the chicken breast at 400°f for 15 minutes, then let cool and dice.
grill the chicken over robata coals for an intense smoky flavor, set aside.
in a medium saucepan, combine the matcha-infused coconut milk, heavy cream, and granulated sugar. whisk to combine.
cook the mixture over medium heat, whisking constantly, until it thickens slightly and reaches 170°f.
remove the saucepan from the heat and stir in the egg yolks, matcha powder, and melted butter, whisking until well combined.
let the mixture cool slightly, then stir in the diced chicken, chopped cilantro, lime juice, and red pepper flakes.
pour the mixture into 8-10 small ramekins or custard cups.
place the ramekins in a large baking dish and add hot water to come halfway up the sides of the ramekins.
bake in a preheated oven at 350°f for 25-30 minutes, or until the edges are set and the centers are still slightly jiggly.
remove the ramekins from the water bath and let cool to room temperature.
cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours, or until chilled and set.
